title: Deliciously Crusted Argentinian Millet Hash
description: A spicy, shaggy, and comforting solo-dinner featuring the flavors of Argentina, carefully made without onions and garlic.
instructions: In a large cast-iron skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the ground chorizo and cook, breaking it up with a spoon, until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced red bell pepper and cook until tender, about 3 minutes.
Add the millet and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Roll out the lamination puff pastry and cut it into 1-inch squares.
Adding a few squares of puff pastry to the skillet at a time, press them gently into the millet mixture, making sure to cover the entire surface.
Sprinkle the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper over the top of the millet mixture.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the millet is tender.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.
